help: Unified project configuration for florentine-abbot.
This file combines format templates, routing rules,
and metadata configuration into a single file.
Sections:
formats  — filename parsing and archive formatting templates
routes   — pattern-based file routing rules
metadata — tag mapping + multi-language metadata texts
Templates for parsing incoming filenames and formatting archive files.
source_filename_template:
Defines the expected structure of incoming filenames.
Each {field} placeholder becomes a regex capture group for parsing.
Numeric fields (year, month, day, hour, minute, second, sequence) match digits.
Extension matches alphanumeric characters.
All other fields (modifier, group, subgroup, side, suffix) match any
characters up to the next separator.
Examples:
Default: '{year}.{month}.{day}.{hour}.{minute}.{second}.{modifier}.{group}.{subgroup}.{sequence}.{side}.{suffix}.{extension}'
Without time: '{year}.{month}.{day}.{modifier}.{group}.{subgroup}.{sequence}.{side}.{suffix}.{extension}'
Underscore-separated: '{year}_{month}_{day}_{modifier}_{group}_{sequence}.{extension}'
archive_path_template / archive_filename_template:
Define how files are organized and named in the archive structure.
Use Python format specifiers: {month:02d}, {sequence:04d}, etc.
Available fields from parsed filename:
{year}, {month}, {day}, {hour}, {minute}, {second}
{modifier}, {group}, {subgroup}, {sequence}, {side}, {suffix}, {extension}
Archive path examples:
Default (year/date): '{year:04d}/{year:04d}.{month:02d}.{day:02d}'
Flat (date only): '{year:04d}.{month:02d}.{day:02d}'
By month: '{year:04d}/{year:04d}.{month:02d}'
Archive filename examples:
Default: '{year:04d}.{month:02d}.{day:02d}.{hour:02d}.{minute:02d}.{second:02d}.{modifier}.{group}.{subgroup}.{sequence:04d}.{side}.{suffix}'
Compact: '{year:04d}{month:02d}{day:02d}_{hour:02d}{minute:02d}{second:02d}_{group}_{suffix}'
Note: Extension is added automatically and should NOT be included in archive_filename_template.
Pattern-based routing rules for file organization.
Determines which subfolder a file goes into based on its filename.
rules: ordered list of [glob_pattern, subfolder] entries.
Each filename is tested against the patterns in order — first match wins.
Patterns use standard glob syntax: * (any chars), ? (single char), [seq] (char set).
Matching is case-insensitive.
Subfolder values:
'SOURCES': Files go to SOURCES/ subfolder
'DERIVATIVES': Files go to DERIVATIVES/ subfolder
'.': Files go directly to date folder root
Any other string: custom subfolder name
Metadata configuration: tag mapping and multi-language texts.
tags: maps friendly field names to XMP tags written to image files.
To add custom fields:
1. Add a new entry in tags: "fieldname": "XMP-namespace:TagName"
2. Add the field to your languages below
languages: keys are BCP-47 codes like 'ru-RU', 'en-US'.
Exactly one language SHOULD have 'default': true.
Field types:
creator — string or array of strings (author names)
Other fields — string or array of strings (multi-line text)
